title: Faster-than-Light Propulsion
content:
Faster-than-light (also known as FTL or Superluminal) travel refers to the various methods used to traverse immense distances in a reasonable amount of time. The development of FTL travel by a civilization is a major milestone, typically serving as the dividing line for a civilization entering the "interstellar era". Without faster-than-light propulsion, interstellar communication, trade, and travel would be impossible within the life spans of most species in the galaxy. Starships without FTL capabilities are usually refered to as slow ships, slower-than-light or sublight ships, or sleeper ships; the latter refering to the use of cyrogenic sleep systems that enable a crew of a slow ship to survive the long journey.

FTL Methods

Various methods of FTL travel have been developed across the galaxy by different species, with the main archetypes being the following:

  • Warp Drive: The most common method of FTL travel, which involves distorting spacetime around a vessel to enable it to move through real-space at superluminal speeds. Early warp drives often faced the issue of producing a gamma-ray burst in front of the vessel; potentially causing harm to anything in front of the ship, or in some cases, to the ship itself. Notable examples of warp drives are the Apleen's Ourantan Device and Humanity's Spool Drive.
  • Jump/Instantaneous Drive: The rarest form of FTL travel (due to the limitations of traditional physics) a jump drive transports a vessel through real space instantaneously. Precise use of jump drives require extensive sensor data of the target location, and as such these drives typically have a shorter jump range, limited to a ship's long range sensors. The most notable form jump drive was developed by Equestrians - the Teleportation Megaspell.
  • Wormhole Generator: Another rare form of FTL, wormhole generators do as their name implies. Wormhole generators are often lumped in with jump drives, due to the instantaneous travel inside a wormhole. However, a ship must travel a short distance in real space to enter and exit a wormhole, and thus are not true jump drives. A large enough wormhole can enable an entire fleet to move in formation, instantaneously. However, wormhole generators are large, require extreme amounts of power, and are infamously unreliable with the exact endpoint of a wormhole, which has led to their relatively uncommon usage as FTL propulsion. Wormhole generators were often used by the military of United Equus and its successor state, the Solar Empire.
  • Slipspace Drive: Also known as slipdrives, this method is the fastest (aside from jump drives) and most efficient form of FTL travel currently known to exist. Slipspace drives operate by pushing a vessel into a higher dimension known as slipspace, where it can then move at extreme speeds without signifigant power expenditure. However, travel in slipspace is limited by natural filaments called slipspace routes, as vessels are only able to move via these routes. While it is theorized that every location in the galaxy is connected to a route, the varying intsensity of the filaments limit the realistic reach of slipspace. Even the most basic slipspace drives are able to access the strongest intensity routes, while less intense routes can only be accessed by progressively more complex and energy-demanding drives. As such, slipdrives are typically relegated as the secondary drive for vessels, useful only for long distance travel, while the first and final legs of a journey are completed via traditional FTL methods.
